title: Bridge Essay: Auditory hallucinations in schizophrenia are associated with reduced functional

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Language/Mantra bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**Auditory hallucinations in schizophrenia are associated with reduced functional connectivity of the temporo-parietal area.** — Biological psychiatry (2010). PMID: 20060103. DOI: 10.1016/j.biopsych.2009.11.017. Score: 33. Schizophrenia has been conceptualized as adisorder of integration of neural activity across distributed networks. However, the relationship between specific symptom dimensions and patterns of functional connectivity remains unclear. The current study aimed to investigate the relationship between auditory-verbal hallucinations (AVH), aparticularly prevalent and clinically relevant symptom in schizophrenia, and functional connectivity of the temporo-parietal junction (TPJ).
